mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls is a coincident indicator of aggregate income and demand; higher PAYEMS levels directly translate into consumption-driven corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS level versus 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 0.5% marks the start of an explicit tightening cycle, raising the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and compressing equity multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts captures broad-based demand strength across both goods and residential investment; this joint demand impulse raises top-line revenue visibility for cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST adds no incremental explanatory power over individual series in a 20-day forward return regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during a high-unemployment recovery signals rapid labor market repair that outpaces wage-cost pressures, supporting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over prior 3 months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive following UNRATE declines greater than 0.2 percentage points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5% month-over-month indicates accelerating manufacturing utilization and supply-side momentum, which supports earnings growth for industrial and materials sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP above its trailing four-quarter trend level signals persistent demand-driven earnings momentum; as macroeconomic surprises diffuse into equity prices, this generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 is above its four-quarter trend (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation above a 3% annualized three-month pace triggers fears of monetary policy repricing and erodes real earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales levels trending above their six-month moving average reflect durable consumer demand strength that supports discretionary and staples revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative on average when RSAFS is above its six-month trend (t-stat < -1).
